summary_one_line: Reaching yy_destructor's true side needs deep SQL parser reductions (SELECT-prefixed grammar) that exercise the destructor under a specific call context.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A syntactically rich SQL input (leading SELECT keyword followed by nested parser productions) that drives the Lemon parser through deep yy_reduce reductions, so yy_destructor is invoked from a destructor-bearing grammar symbol and takes the true branch at line 155710.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal bytes 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) and contain repeated '(' and '$' structure that drives many parser reductions; the blocker true side fires (L155710 T=8 in W) and yy_reduce executes 2680 false / hundreds of true reductions only in W, reaching the destructor.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed fragments (15-79 bytes) that do not begin with SELECT and never drive the parser into the deep reduction paths; the blocker true side is never taken (L155710 T=0 in L) and yy_reduce sees only 61 reductions.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same yy_destructor edge recorded under a new (call-context, edge) pair counts as new coverage; this rewards inputs that reach yy_destructor via novel parse-reduction call paths, which plain naive collapses into one already-seen edge and so never prioritizes. Both fuzzers share identical havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the only enabling difference is coverage granularity.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx winning over naive with delta ctx_coverage.
naive_ctx resolves the branch in 9/10 trials while naive blocks in all 10.
The blocker is in the Lemon parser destructor function yy_destructor.
All winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal SELECT keyword bytes.
Winner bytes at offsets 0x00-0x05 are concentrated on SELECT while loser bytes are scattered fragments.
The loser-blocking seeds are short malformed fragments that do not start with SELECT.
The blocker true side is taken in W but not in L.
Deep parser reduction (yy_reduce) executes vastly more in W than L, evidencing deeper parse paths only in the winner.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds call context into the edge-map index so a new (call-context, edge) pair is new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge

evidence_trail.claim: Both involved fuzzers use the same havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so a lineage dash-row query is not mandatory; the MECHANISM CONTEXT section directly confirms naive_ctx uses a CtxHook that records (call-context, edge) pairs and that both involved fuzzers share havoc/token mutators with no I2S, so the only enabling difference is coverage granularity rather than comparison-solving.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which the destructor-bearing branch is reachable via only one fixed call context shows naive resolving it as fast as naive_ctx, indicating context granularity (not parse depth) was not the enabling factor.

weakest_evidence_point: The link from 'SELECT-prefixed grammar drives deep reductions' to the specific yymajor token that makes yy_destructor take its true side is inferred from call-chain counts, not from a confirmed grammar-symbol-to-branch mapping.
